description: Patch: Codex sub-agent collab-tool hard disable (#240/#241), trellis-start update-path fix, Kiro CLI schema migration (#247), Codex agent template drift fix, Windows Python fallback chain (#236).
changelog:
**Bug Fixes:**
- fix(codex): disable Codex collab tools in sub-agent role files at protocol level (#240, #241). 0.5.5 removed the SessionStart injection vector, 0.5.6 added prompt-layer `fork_turns="none"` guidance — but [Ca11back's reproduction on #241](https://github.com/mindfold-ai/Trellis/issues/241) showed the main agent still spawned with default `fork_turns="all"`, the child inherited the parent's `spawn_agent(...)` records, applied the "wait for spawned subagents" rule to itself, and `wait_agent`-stuck on phantom children. Structural fix: each `codex/agents/trellis-{implement,check,research}.toml` now sets `[features].multi_agent = false` and `[features.multi_agent_v2].enabled = false`. Codex's `tool_config.rs:179-180` gates `spawn_agent` / `wait_agent` / `list_agents` / `close_agent` registration on both flags being on; with both off the tools don't exist in the sub-agent's tool list — the model literally cannot call them. Codex agent role files support full ConfigToml override layers (`codex-rs/core/src/config/agent_roles.rs:217-225`), `[features]` included.
- fix(codex): write `trellis-start` skill on update path, not just init. 0.5.5's `configureCodex()` writes `.agents/skills/trellis-start/SKILL.md` so the `<trellis-bootstrap>` notice from `inject-workflow-state.py` resolves to a real skill — but `collectPlatformTemplates.codex.collectTemplates()` (used by `trellis update`) was missed. Result: users upgrading from 0.4.x → 0.5.5/0.5.6 ran the safe-file-delete migration that removed `.agents/skills/start/`, then `trellis update` regenerated all the other trellis-* skill dirs from `collectTemplates` — but never wrote `trellis-start`. Their AI then reported "no .agents/skills/trellis-start/SKILL.md" on every turn that hit `<trellis-bootstrap>`. Extracted `resolveCodexTrellisStartSkill()` helper in `configurators/shared.ts`, called from both paths so the file shows up on init and update.
- fix(kiro): migrate agent JSON to Kiro CLI's current schema (#247). Kiro CLI rejected Trellis's pre-0.5.7 agent JSON with "invalid agent". Three schema changes per [Agent Configuration Reference](https://kiro.dev/docs/cli/custom-agents/configuration-reference): (1) `instructions` → `prompt`; (2) adds `allowedTools` field (mirrors `tools` — Kiro splits "available" from "permitted"); (3) `hooks` switched from array of `{on, command, timeout_ms}` entries to object keyed by event name (`hooks: { agentSpawn: [{ command: "..." }] }`). Affects all three trellis-{implement,check,research}.json files.
- fix(codex): port "Load Trellis Context First" section back to template (drift). The Codex sub-agent role files in `.codex/agents/` (project dogfood) had a 27-line section telling the sub-agent how to find its active task path on class-2 platforms — but the canonical template files in `packages/cli/src/templates/codex/agents/trellis-{implement,check}.toml` were missing it. Drift went undetected: dogfood was richer than what `trellis init` shipped to user projects. Fix: copy the section into both template files. `trellis-research.toml` was already in sync.
- fix(init): Windows Python detection fallback chain (#236). `trellis init` previously tried only `python --version` on Windows. If the host had Python under `python3` (Microsoft Store) or `py -3` (python.org launcher) but not `python`, init failed outright with `Python command "python" not found`. Workaround was to install 0.4.0 first then upgrade. Now `resolveSupportedPython()` walks `python → python3 → py -3` on Windows (`python3 → python` elsewhere) and uses the first candidate that probes Python ≥ 3.9. The resolved command is cached via `setResolvedPythonCommand()` in `configurators/shared.ts` so all downstream template / configurator writes pick up the same value. Adds `TRELLIS_PYTHON_CMD=<cmd>` (explicit override) and respects pre-existing `TRELLIS_SKIP_PYTHON_CHECK=1`. Failure case throws an aggregated error with a Windows-specific install hint.
notes: Patch on top of 0.5.6. Run `trellis update` (no `--migrate` needed). Codex users get hardened sub-agent role files (collab tools structurally disabled) plus the previously-init-only `trellis-start` skill on the update path. Kiro users get an agent JSON that Kiro CLI no longer rejects. Windows users who previously had to install 0.4.0 first can now run `trellis init` directly on 0.5.7+.
